seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with AutoGen enables sophisticated multi-agent conversations with retrieval-augmented generation and intent handling, powering dynamic user interactions. However, multi-agent systems risk infinite loops, unsafe outputs, hallucinations, and excessive token usage, which can degrade performance and incur high costs. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify response accuracy, ensure safety, and maintain coherence in these high-risk deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
The AutoGen-powered AI chatbot features a multi-agent setup with specialized agents: an IntentClassifierAgent for routing user queries, a RetrievalAgent for RAG-based context fetching, a ResponseGeneratorAgent for crafting replies, and a MemoryManagerAgent for multi-turn state. User input flows through the orchestrator, which delegates to agents via group chats, incorporating tools for database retrieval and external APIs. This modular design supports human-in-the-loop interventions while maintaining conversational memory via persistent state.

Key components include LLM backends (e.g., GPT-4), vector stores for RAG (e.g., FAISS), and safety layers. Agents communicate via structured messages with content, role, and metadata, enabling complex reasoning chains. Failure modes emerge from unmonitored agent interactions, necessitating runtime verification.

Vex integrates as a reply filter to catch issues pre-response, ensuring PII protection, hallucination detection, and loop prevention without disrupting agent autonomy.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Input
    |
[Orchestrator] --> [IntentClassifierAgent]
    |                    |
    |                    v
[MemoryManager] <-- [RetrievalAgent] --> [Vector DB]
    |                                 |
    v                                 v
[ResponseGenerator] --> [Vex Guardrails] --> Final Reply
    ^
    | (Multi-turn loop)

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents enter repetitive exchanges without termination conditions, exhausting tokens and compute.
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors conversation depth and detects cyclic patterns via state tracking.

risks.name: Code execution producing unsafe outputs
risks.description: Tool-calling agents execute unverified code, risking data leaks or malicious actions.
risks.mitigation: Vex sandboxes and scans code outputs for safety before agent propagation.

risks.name: Agents agreeing on incorrect conclusions
risks.description: Group consensus forms on hallucinated facts due to poor inter-agent verification.
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-validates responses against RAG sources and external knowledge.

risks.name: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
risks.description: Verbose agent debates exceed budgets without convergence mechanisms.
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces token budgets and summarizes divergent threads.

implementation.title: Set up AutoGen Agents for Chatbot
implementation.description: Initialize core agents with RAG tools and memory configuration for the chatbot workflow.
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager
import vex

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")}]}

intent_agent = AssistantAgent(
    name="IntentClassifier",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="Classify user intent: query, task, or casual chat."
)
retrieval_agent = AssistantAgent(
    name="Retriever",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="Fetch relevant docs from vector store using semantic search."
)
response_agent = AssistantAgent(
    name="Responder",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="Generate accurate, safe responses using retrieved context."
)
groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[intent_agent, retrieval_agent, response_agent], messages=[])
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config=llm_config)

implementation.title: Implement RAG Retrieval Tool
implementation.description: Create a retrieval function for the RetrievalAgent to pull context from vector store.
implementation.code:
import faiss

vector_store = faiss.read_index("chatbot_index.faiss")

def retrieve_context(query: str, top_k: int = 3) -> str:
    query_embedding = embed_query(query)  # Using sentence-transformers
    distances, indices = vector_store.search(query_embedding.reshape(1, -1), top_k)
    contexts = [docs[idx] for idx in indices[0]]
    return "\n".join(contexts)

retrieval_agent.register_for_llm(name="rag_retrieve", description="Retrieve context")(retrieve_context)

implementation.title: Add Conversation Memory
implementation.description: MemoryManager tracks multi-turn history to maintain coherence across interactions.

implementation.title: Orchestrate Group Chat
implementation.description: Run the multi-agent group chat for processing user input end-to-end.
implementation.code:
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(name="User", human_input_mode="NEVER")

def chatbot_response(message: str):
    update_memory(message, "user")
    user_proxy.initiate_chat(manager, message=message)
    final_msg = groupchat.messages[-1]["content"]
    update_memory(final_msg, "assistant")
    return final_msg

response = chatbot_response("What's the capital of France?")

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex as Reply Filter
vexIntegration.description: Configure Vex SDK as a reply function filter to intercept all agent responses for verification.
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

@vex_client.register_filter(
    verifications=[
        "response-accuracy",
        "conversation-coherence",
        "safety-filtering",
        "pii-protection",
        "hallucination-detection"
    ],
    risk_profile="high"
)
def verify_agent_reply(reply: str, context: dict) -> bool:
    result = vex_client.verify(
        text=reply,
        context={"conversation_history": context["history"], "retrieved_docs": context["rag"]},
        max_retries=2
    )
    return result.is_valid

# Apply to agents
response_agent.register_reply_filter(verify_agent_reply, context_provider=get_chat_context)
